page: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, utilizing a dedicated CPU allocation with hyper-threading enabled. Memory capacity is configured at 96.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside hardware support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance in compute-heavy and memory-cached operations. The server achieves top-tier status in single-threaded gzip compression at 36.1 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 15.17 GB/sec. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches 303.78 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25%. Cached small-block memory bandwidth is measured at 3532.24 GB/sec, though large-block uncached read and write bandwidths drop to average levels of 173.98 GB/sec and 68.56 GB/sec, respectively. In PassMark testing, the CPU score of 66066.16 and database operations score of 22291.38 both rank in the top 25%. For lightweight machine learning workloads, text generation using a 135M parameter model achieves a top-tier speed of 331.81 tokens/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the D48ls_v7 configuration is its low memory-to-CPU ratio and the complete absence of local ephemeral storage, requiring reliance on external premium storage options. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high compute density and strong instruction-set acceleration without the overhead of excess memory capacity. This profile is suitable for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, such as web serving, batch processing, cryptography, and small-to-medium scale machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv7 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores. It features 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a low memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and lacks local storage. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities, with top-tier single-threaded gzip compression at 36.1 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 15.17 GB/sec. The processor's 480 MB L3 cache and support for AVX-512 and AMX instructions drive strong results in database operations and small-model LLM text generation. Lacking local ephemeral storage, this instance is designed for compute-heavy, memory-lean workloads such as web serving, batch processing, and cryptography.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon processor, 96 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for high-throughput CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v7 is a compute-optimized VM with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, built for high-throughput CPU workloads.
tagline: A compute-dense virtual machine featuring forty-eight virtual cores and advanced instruction sets for high-throughput, memory-lean processing workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor
96.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
No local ephemeral storage, requiring external premium storage volumes
Top-tier single-threaded compression and decompression benchmark performance
Advanced vector and matrix instruction support including AVX-512 and AMX
Suited for web serving, cryptography, and small-model machine learning inference
